# Twyne

> Twyne is a local-first writing desk with a "room of editors" in residence.
> You file a short brief about what you're writing, then draft while AI personas
> read along, leave marginalia on your sentences, and grade the draft against a
> rubric. Your work is stored in your browser by default and only syncs when you
> sign in. The AI is bring-your-own-key: you choose the provider and model.

## What Twyne does

- The Dossier: a short brief interview captures what you're writing and for whom.
- The Room of Editors: AI personas review your draft and leave inline notes.
- The Galley Proof: a rubric grades the draft as you write.
- The Marginalia: persona comments anchored to the sentences they're about.
- The Apparatus: research and bibliography tools while you write.
- Folios: drafts, notes, and outlines; export as Markdown, HTML, text, or JSON.

## Key facts

- Local-first: drafts live in the browser (IndexedDB); sign-in enables sync.
- Bring-your-own-key (BYOK): API keys stay local and call the provider directly.
- Default AI: the latest Claude models from Anthropic; other providers supported.
- Auth: email, one-time codes, passkeys, and Bluesky / ATProto sign-in.
- Publishing: drafts can be published to an ATProto / Bluesky PDS.

## Docs

- The Manual (full guide): https://twyne.love/docs/
- FAQ: https://twyne.love/faq/

- Privacy Policy: https://twyne.love/privacy/
